verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (hK2) is a secreted serine protease expressed predominantly in prostate tissue. It is processed through the classical secretory pathway and released into extracellular fluids. There is no intrinsic TM domain or GPI anchor. However, KLK2 is detected on the surface of prostate cancer cells via association with cell-surface binding partners and has been targeted by antibody-based radioimmunoconjugates and ADC approaches engaging it on prostate cancer cell surfaces. This surface presence is tissue/cell-type restricted (prostate cancer) and partner-dependent, fitting contextual / cell_state_induced.
